mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, which would lead to higher interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, which would lead to higher wages and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the past 6 months indicates rising supply chain pressures, which would lead to higher costs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) over the past 3 months, combined with a 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the same period, signals a strong economy with low unemployment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strong economy periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) over the past 3 months, combined with a 0.5% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) over the same period, signals an easing monetary cycle with rising inflation expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: easing monetary cycle with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Nonfarm Payroll Employment (PAYEMS) over the past 3 months, combined with a 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the same period, signals a strong labor market with low unemployment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: strong labor market with low unemployment
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strong labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Housing Starts (HOUST) over the past 6 months indicates rising housing demand, which would lead to higher construction costs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.
